weaknesses: DIT annotation methodology lacks detail on annotator identity, inter-annotator agreement, and validation procedure
Task selection criteria not explained -- unclear whether 84 tasks represent a principled sample or convenience collection
Cost multipliers presented as averages without per-task variance or confidence intervals
No wall-clock timing or hardware details (acceptable for API-based experiments but may draw reviewer questions)
Majority-class baseline description is thin -- does not specify which topology wins majority-class selection in the training data
revision_priority: Add 1-2 sentences on DIT annotation methodology (who annotated, agreement metric)
Clarify task selection criteria or sampling strategy
Specify which topology the majority-class baseline selects
Consider adding a sentence on API call latency if wall-clock timing data is available
